name: Pico O Mostallar – circular from Piornedo – Sierra de Ancares
name_original: Pico O Mostallar – circular desde Piornedo – Sierra de Ancares
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Ancares traverse a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve characterized by deep valleys and towering peaks, offering a varied natural landscape. The region features dense oak, chestnut, and beech forests on lower slopes, transitioning to birch formations and heathlands at higher altitudes. Rivers like the Ancares, Burbia, and Cúa have shaped these valleys, providing diverse routes for exploration. This environment supports a wide array of plant species and offers a glimpse into ancient culture through traditional "pallozas." Best easy hiking trails in Ancares The most popular easy hiking route is Cantejeira Waterfall Trail, a 1.9 miles (3.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This path leads to a scenic waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cantejeira Waterfall loop from Cantejeira, an easy 1.9 miles (3.1 km) path. This loop offers views of the waterfall and surrounding forest. Local hikers also love the Bar La Laguna – Seated Hiker Statue loop from La Laguna, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking in Ancares is defined by deep valleys, dense oak and chestnut forests, and picturesque rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, including many easy hiking trails. The routes in Ancares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Ancares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Ancares?
A: Ancares offers a variety of hiking experiences, with &lt;strong&gt;3 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; specifically curated for those seeking gentle walks. In total, the region features 11 hiking trails across various difficulty levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any easy trails that lead to waterfalls in Ancares?
A: Yes, you can enjoy an easy hike to a beautiful waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050748926/cantejeira-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantejeira Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 1.9-mile (3.1 km) path that takes about an hour to complete, leading directly to a scenic waterfall. For a circular option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantejeira Waterfall loop from Cantejeira&lt;/a&gt;, which also offers views of the waterfall and surrounding forest.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in Ancares?
A: Absolutely. Many easy trails in Ancares are designed as loops. Besides the waterfall loop, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20495597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar La Laguna – Seated Hiker Statue loop from La Laguna&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.9-mile (4.6 km) trail through varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vega de Valcarce – Roman Bridge at Las Herrerías loop from Vega de Valcarce&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4 miles (6.5 km) long and offers historical sights.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see on an easy hike in Ancares?
A: Ancares is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot deer, various bird species like golden eagles and peregrine falcons, and if you're very lucky, elusive brown bears or wolves. The diverse environment, from dense forests to heathlands, supports a wide array of flora and fauna.

Q: Are there family-friendly easy hikes suitable for children in Ancares?
A: Many of the easy trails are suitable for families, offering manageable distances and gentle elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050748926/cantejeira-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantejeira Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is short and rewarding, making it a great option for children. The region's varied landscapes and cultural sites also provide engaging points of interest for younger hikers.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore on an easy hike in Ancares?
A: Ancares is rich in history. You can encounter traditional 'pallozas' – circular stone huts with thatched roofs – in villages like Piornedo or O Cebreiro, some of which are preserved as ethnographic museums. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of O Cebreiro – Along the Way loop from O Cebreiro&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to experience a section of the historic Camino de Santiago and visit the significant stop of O Cebreiro. You might also find remnants of medieval castles or prehistoric sites.

Q: Are there viewpoints or scenic spots on easy trails in Ancares?
A: Yes, even on easy trails, you can find stunning vistas. The region is known for its deep valleys and towering peaks. Trails around O Cebreiro, for instance, offer panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. While higher peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mostallar&lt;/a&gt; are more challenging to summit, their presence shapes the dramatic scenery visible from many easier paths.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ancares?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Ancares, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's stunning and varied natural landscape, the tranquility of its ancient woodlands, and the authentic cultural experience offered by its traditional villages and historical sites. The well-preserved natural beauty and rich biodiversity are frequently highlighted.

Q: How long do easy Ancares hikes typically take?
A: Easy hikes in Ancares generally range from 1 to 2 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050748926/cantejeira-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantejeira Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20495597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar La Laguna – Seated Hiker Statue loop from La Laguna&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 1 hour 20 minutes. Distances vary from 1.9 miles (3.1 km) to about 4 miles (6.5 km).

Q: What should I wear for easy Ancares hikes, especially in autumn?
A: For easy hikes in Ancares, especially in autumn, it's best to dress in layers. The weather can be changeable, with cooler mornings and evenings, and potential for rain. We recommend waterproof hiking boots, comfortable socks, moisture-wicking base layers, a fleece or insulated mid-layer, and a waterproof and windproof outer shell. A hat and gloves can also be useful.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads for easy hikes in Ancares?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at or near the trailheads for many easy hikes in Ancares, particularly in or near villages like Cantejeira, La Laguna, Vega de Valcarce, and O Cebreiro. These areas are often starting points for popular routes and are equipped to accommodate visitors.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Ancares?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Ancares is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder and more stable, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant autumn colors later in the year. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide a more temperate experience with fewer crowds.
tips: Located at an altitude of 1,935 meters, Pico del Mostallar or Mustallar is the highest and most emblematic peak in Los Ancares and in the entire province of Lugo.

Pico dos Lagos, also known as Pico Lanza, is a mountain in the Sierra de Ancares. With just over 2,000 meters of altitude, the peak rises to the north of Puebla de Lillo, right on the border between the communities of Galicia and León.
